items.acceptanceCriteria: Equipment documentation is available
Previous inspection reports are checked
Daily pre-operational inspection checklists are checked (if applicable)
Preventive Maintenance is established and updated records are maintained
Rated load is clearly marked and visible to the operator (Arabic & English)
S/R machine is painted safety yellow
Warning and cautionary labels are affixed at aisle entrance points or access positions and are durable and legible
A sign is posted warning the operator not to rely solely on any automatic device as a substitute for safe operating practice
Clearances and tolerances within the system are as determined by the manufacturer or user (specifications)
A fire extinguisher with minimum 10BC rating is available (in the cab)
Welded members and joints are free of defects, cracks and corrosion
Structures and supports of S/R machine are not cracked , corroded or deformed
Structures and supports of S/R machine are free of unusual vibrations
S/R machine rails are straight, leveled and properly joined
Stops are provided at the limits of travel of the S/R machine and aisle transfer car
Structure and S/R machine shows no loose bolts or rivets.
All devices/controls required for operation are within convenient reach of operator
The cab interior is free of knobs, edges or corners
The cab door, if fitted, opens inward or slides and is self-closing with a positive latch
Emergency exits to the floor are available for all positions of a carriage mounted cab
All cab glazing is safety glazing material
Cab lighting to be adequate (either natural or artificial ) to enable the operator observe the controls
All ladders and platforms are secure and not corroded or damaged
Ladder access opening to platforms is 24" x 27" with hinged cover (610mm x 685mm)
Platforms have non-slip walking surfaces
Bumpers provide required stop of an S/R machine or aisle transfer car travelling at 100% rated load and speed from causing structural damage to the equipment
Runway interlocks are provided to prevent travel between the aisle and aisle transfer car unless the tracks are aligned
Sweeps are fitted in front of the runway wheels
Guards for hoisting ropes or chains are fitted where appropriate to prevent chafing
Guards are fitted over moving parts such as gears ,sprockets ,chains and ropes where these constitute a hazard

Holding brake exists (at least one) for each independent hoisting unit of the S/R machine (125% full load hoisting torque for non-mechanical brake and 100% for a mechanical one - holding brake shall be applied automatically when power to the brake is removed)
Holding brake is applied automatically when power to the brake is removed
Control braking is capable of maintaining controlled travel or lowering speeds
Wearing surfaces of brake wheels, disks and drums are free of defects that could interfere with their operation
The electrical cables outside of control enclosures are fully protected and insulated (S/R machine or transfer car)
Traveling cables are suspended at the carriage and S/R machine frame end as to reduce the strain on the individual conductors
Supporting fillers are used for unsuspended travelling cable lengths exceeding 100ft (30m)
The entire S/R machine is electrically grounded
Any pendant control station is electrically grounded
Live parts of electrical equipment are protected from direct exposure to grease, oil, dirt and moisture
Any guards fitted over live parts are not deformed
Power disconnect between the power supply and the aisle contact conductor or travelling cable is provided (motor circuit switch or breaker)
Operation of limit sensors, which shut down any drive whose motion passes the extremity of designed travel, is satisfactory
Operation of limit sensors where used to reduce speed prior to the machine reaching the extreme travel limit is satisfactory
Hoist motion overspeed device operate independently from all other power, drive and electrical systems (carriage mounted cab only)
The overspeed device shall operate whenever the lowering rate exceeds a maximum of 150% of the rated lowering speed.
Overspeed switch operation to stop descent of the carriage
Actual overspeed figure at which the device is set to operate is clearly marked on the device in letters and figures not less than 1Ú4 in. (6 mm)
Overspeed device is sealed to prevent readjustment of the trip speed
Control voltages do not exceed 150V AC or 300V DC
Controls at operator's cab are within reach of the operator (for arms and legs)
Sequence of operation for the controls is verified (automatic control operating sequence)
Audio and visual warning devices are operable
Emergency stop switch(es) are in good working condition
Electrical overload or power failure sensors are fitted
Emergency stop actuator(s) in the aisle(s) are operable

Correct sequence of operation under automatic and remote control of S/R machine and aisle transfer car is verified (In auto mode all motion is discontinued if the sequence is interrupted, or the last command is permissible if power is available. In remote mode if the signal is interrupted the machine stops)
Sheave grooves are smooth with no surface defects
Close fitting rope guides or guards are fitted where required to prevent momentary unloading of the rope
Sheaves have means of lubrication or are permanently lubricated
Sheave pitch diameter is not less than 20 times the rope diameter
Rope drums are free from surface defects that could cause rope damage
Two wraps of rope remains on the drum (as a minimum) when the carriage is in the extreme low position
Rope is correctly clamped to the drum (or with a socket arrangement) as per the rope or S/R machine manufacturers recommendations

Removal criteria for rope replacement • In running ropes, 12 randomly distributed broken wires in one lay or 4 broken wires in one strand in one lay” • Wear of one-third the original diameter of outside individual wires • kinking, crushing, birdcaging, or any other damage resulting in the distortion of the rope structure • Evidence of heat damage from any cause • Reductions from the nominal diameter greater than 5%.
Sprocket wheels and chain pockets are free from surface defects
Sprockets, pocket wheels or running chains are adequately lubricated.
All lines, tanks, valves, pumps, motors and other parts of fluid systems are not leaking
Bearings, shafts, gears and rollers are not worn, cracked or distorted
Carriage free fall stops are in place (can be activated mechanically by simulating a slack rope or chain condition)
Lifting and lowering function of the cab and carriage is satisfactory
Horizontal travel function of the machine is satisfactory
Shuttle function of the machine is satisfactory
All moving parts of the S/R machine or aisle transfer car for which lubrication is specified, including rope and chain, are lubricated
